name: Fixed Sliding Window
summary: Keep a window of length |p| and a running letter-count of it. Slide one step at a time — add the entering letter, drop the leaving one — and record every position where the window's counts equal p's.
visualizationIntent: init: show s with an empty target map and window map. update: prime both maps from p and the first window of s. check: compare the window map to the target map — if equal, flash the window green and record its start index. move: slide right by one — the entering letter joins the window map, the leaving letter is decremented (and dropped if it hits zero). return: show all matching start indices.

lineExplanations.3: Start an empty list of answer indices.
lineExplanations.4: If the pattern is longer than the string, no anagram can exist.
lineExplanations.6: The target letter-counts we are looking for (from p).
lineExplanations.8: Index used to prime both maps over the first |p| characters.
lineExplanations.10: Take the pattern character at this index.
lineExplanations.11: Have we already counted this pattern letter?
lineExplanations.14: No — start its target count at 1.
lineExplanations.15: Take the string character at this index — the first window.
lineExplanations.16: Have we already counted this window letter?
lineExplanations.19: No — start its window count at 1.
lineExplanations.21: The window's left edge starts at index 0.
lineExplanations.22: The window's right edge is the last index of the first window.
lineExplanations.23: Do the window counts already equal the target counts?
lineExplanations.24: If so, the very first window is an anagram.
lineExplanations.26: Slide the window until its right edge reaches the end of s.
lineExplanations.27: Reset the match flag for this new position.
lineExplanations.28: Extend the right edge by one.
lineExplanations.29: The letter entering the window on the right.
lineExplanations.30: Is this letter already in the window map?
lineExplanations.34: The letter leaving the window on the left.
lineExplanations.36: Did that letter's count drop to zero?
lineExplanations.37: Remove it — a zero-count letter must not linger, or the maps could never match.
lineExplanations.38: Advance the left edge to complete the slide.
lineExplanations.39: Do the window counts now equal the target counts?
lineExplanations.40: If so, this window is an anagram.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def findAnagrams(self, s, p): s is searched for anagrams of p.
syntaxExplanations.4: len(p) > len(s) — a longer pattern cannot fit anywhere in s.
syntaxExplanations.5: return res exits early with the empty list.
syntaxExplanations.9: while i < len(p): runs once per pattern character.
syntaxExplanations.10: p[i] reads the i-th pattern character.
syntaxExplanations.11: pc in need is True if the dictionary already has that key.
syntaxExplanations.12: need[pc] + 1 reads the old count and stores one more.
syntaxExplanations.13: else: covers a brand-new pattern letter.
syntaxExplanations.14: need[pc] = 1 creates the key with count 1.
syntaxExplanations.15: s[i] reads the i-th character of s — part of the first window.
syntaxExplanations.16: sc in window checks the window map for that key.
syntaxExplanations.18: else: covers a brand-new window letter.
syntaxExplanations.19: window[sc] = 1 creates the key with count 1.
syntaxExplanations.20: i = i + 1 advances the priming index.
syntaxExplanations.21: left = 0 sets the window's left edge.
syntaxExplanations.22: len(p) - 1 is the last index of the first window.
syntaxExplanations.23: window == need compares the two dictionaries key-for-key.
syntaxExplanations.24: if matched: is True when the window is an anagram of p.
syntaxExplanations.26: while right < len(s) - 1: slides until the right edge hits the end.
syntaxExplanations.27: matched = False resets the flag before this position is tested.
syntaxExplanations.28: right = right + 1 extends the window on the right.
syntaxExplanations.30: enter in window checks whether it is already counted.
syntaxExplanations.32: else: covers a letter new to the window.
syntaxExplanations.33: window[enter] = 1 creates the key with count 1.
syntaxExplanations.34: s[left] is the character leaving on the left.
syntaxExplanations.36: window[leave] == 0 checks whether the letter is now gone.
syntaxExplanations.37: del window[leave] removes the key entirely.
syntaxExplanations.38: left = left + 1 advances the left edge.
syntaxExplanations.39: window == need re-compares after the slide.
syntaxExplanations.40: if matched: is True when this window is an anagram.
syntaxExplanations.42: return res hands back all the start indices.
